epigraph.quote: We had a glorious, windy supper over looking the little bay.

per_stop.quote: We had sandwiches for lunch near Nescliffe in Shropshire.
we had tea next to Telford's Bridge.
The boys frolicked in the mud.
Simon had intended to get up at 6, but overslept to 6.30.
Pretty Grey Welsh houses down to the waterfront. A nice ferry.
We have driven to Roundwood the highest village in Ireland
Steep stone steps + bridges + stepping stones showing life's journey.
It rained + rained until we reached Nore Valley Bonnet Bridge, Nr Kilkenny at 7pm.
We ate on a dangerous roof top, without a wall around.
The Kings of Munster lived on the Rock of Cashel for 600 years
The campsite house + restaurant is in the stables of a disappeared house. There is a huge, deserted, walled garden nearby.
lunch in a lush field of clover with a beautiful view of wooded valley.
We had a glorious, windy supper over looking the little bay.
A ray fish in a tank allowed us to stroke it.
The drystone walls are covered in grass. Red & purple fuschias, blue, yellow & purple flowers.
Simon came out of the dunes to look at the lovely beach.
We spent a good 2 hours at the Tralee Aquadome, similar to Romsey Rapids, but with lovely steam room too.
Elegant Victorian pier passed.
clean, bright, beer, Irish stew.
Cieran founded it with the help of Prince Diarmid, but Cieran died 7 months later of the Plague.
We rowed through our section of the lake to another, while another, still bigger, lay beyond.
The friendly priest shook hands.
Thomas & Peter played fire ball hockey at midnight on the tennis court
the Solstice was celebrated before even the pyramids.
It was a race down to Dun Laoghaire. Appalling signs through Dublin.
We arrived with less than 15 min to spare, but it looks like the ferry will leave 40 min late, anyway.
the engine died after a strong smell of petrol
